title: Object-Format Transition, Compatibility, and Disaster Defense · Repository Operations Board
summary: Unfamiliar advanced Git graphs, objects, reflogs, undo layers, merges, conflicts, rewrites, backports, stashes, bisects, worktrees, remotes, workflows, signatures, submodules, large repositories, filters, trust, maintenance, object formats, and disaster cases requiring deterministic repository evidence plus explicit real-system transfer limits. Planned artifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across unfamiliar cases, performance tasks, explanations, and root-cause repairs. Learners progre…
objectives: Distinguish direct refs, symbolic refs, pseudorefs, remote-tracking refs, packed refs, and detached HEAD using plumbing evidence.
Capture repository boundaries, object format, refs, worktrees, index state, operation state, and recovery anchors before investigation.
Use rev-parse, cat-file, verify-pack, count-objects, and batch inspection to answer bounded questions without checkout or execution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Plan the cumulative evidence run
steps.instruction: Order the Gita Ref Symbolic Pseudoref evidence cycle for this exam task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Gita Ref Symbolic Pseudoref assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Case G32 is a exam in a disposable repository. the repository stores SHA-256 objects while one integration still assumes 40-character IDs. The learner must predict format evidence separates storage identity from accepted input and displayed output formats, run git rev-parse --show-object-format=storage,input,output, explain storage reports sha256 and the audit parser fails its fixed-width object-ID contract, test this changed condition: restore a bundle into a fresh same-format repository and reject an incompatible toolchain, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve trusted refs, object stores, translation data, manifests, and exact loss accounting. Competency probe: Distinguish direct refs, symbolic refs, pseudorefs, remote-tracking refs, packed refs, and detached HEAD using plumbing evidence.
